trade.summary: Both are major ASEAN economies. Indonesia exports coal, palm oil, minerals, and rubber to Thailandia. Thailandia exports automobiles, electronics, chemicals, and processed food to Indonesia. Both compete in some manufacturing sectors. ASEAN free trade area governs trade.

diplomatic.summary: Both are founding ASEAN members with cooperative and generally smooth relations. They coordinate at ASEAN summits on regional issues including Myanmar, South Cina Sea, and economic integration. Bilateral presidential/prime ministerial meetings occur regularly on the sidelines of ASEAN summits.

military.summary: Military cooperation through ASEAN defence mechanisms (ADMM+). Bilateral exercises have occurred. Thailandia's military governance has not fundamentally disrupted ties with Indonesia. Both cooperate on counter-terrorism and border security.

financial.summary: Thai companies (PTT, Charoen Pokphand, Bangkok Bank) have investments in Indonesia. Indonesian companies have limited presence in Thailandia. Cross-border investment is growing as ASEAN economic integration deepens.

energy.summary: Indonesia supplies coal to Thailandia's power sector. PTT (Thailandia) has upstream energy investments in Indonesia. Some cooperation on biofuels and renewable energy.

scientific.summary: Moderate scientific cooperation through ASEAN frameworks. Joint research in agriculture, marine science, and disaster preparedness. University exchanges are active within ASEAN networks.
